content.content:
commit 5f37b680d0e35bed734a2995499b23a1c7419ecb
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Sat Mar 21 15:12:22 2026 +0800

    [opt/rvv] Add 4x2 tiled GEMM kernel (v4) for conv_HWC_s8 — 3.09x speedup
    
    Introduce nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v4: a 4-channel x 2-pixel tiled
    GEMM kernel using LMUL=M2 with vd4dots, replacing the 2x2/M4 v2 kernel
    for small-channel convolutions (out_tensor_ch <= 64).
    
    Optimization approach:
    - Widen tile from 2x2 (2 channels x 2 pixels, LMUL=M4) to 4x2
      (4 channels x 2 pixels, LMUL=M2)
    - Each inner loop iteration loads 2 pixel columns shared across
      4 weight rows, cutting pixel reload traffic by 50%
    - At VLEN=1024: VLMAX(e8,M2)=256, 3 strip iterations for col=576
      vs v2's 2 iterations with VLMAX(e8,M4)=512, but 25% less total
      memory traffic (3456B vs 4608B per 4 output channels)
    - Reduction epilogue packs 8 vredsum results into VL=4 vectors for
      requantization — 2x better VL utilization than v2's VL=2
    - Register allocation: 8 M2 accumulators (v0-v15), 6 M2 load
      buffers (v16-v27), scratch (v28-v31) — all 32 registers used
    
    Also includes v4_pf and v2_pf variants with software prefetch
    (Zicbop prefetch.r), benchmarked but not used — AX45MPV hardware
    prefetcher handles the sequential access pattern with no benefit
    from software hints.
    
    FPGA results (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024, conv_HWC_s8 perf test):
      Initial (vectorize across channels): 5,456,391 cycles
      v2 (vd4dots along reduction dim):    2,208,698 cycles (2.47x)
      v4 (4x2 tiling, LMUL=M2):           1,767,895 cycles (3.09x)
      v4_pf (v4 + prefetch):              1,769,007 cycles (no gain)
      v2_pf (v2 + prefetch):              2,194,205 cycles (no gain)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Include/riscv_nn_support.h                         |   39 +
 .../riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c     |  155 ++-
 .../nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset.c                 | 1056 ++++++++++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 1247 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

Interesting — `_v2`'s `#else` path is just a stub returning `NULL`. It's VEC-only, same as `_v4`. Neither has a plain-C fallback.

So replacing `_v2` calls with `_v4` is straightforward — they have the same signature, same `#else` stub behavior, and `_v4` is strictly better when VEC is enabled. ---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"

content.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text:
QEMU is exiting with code 1 and no output. This seems like a QEMU environment issue (perhaps missing display libraries or something sandbox-related), not related to the code changes. The key facts:

1. **Build succeeded** — all source compiled with `-Wall -Werror`, no errors
2. **QEMU is not producing output** — it exits with code 1 silently, which appears to be an environment issue (the same would happen on unmodified code)

Let me verify this is not caused by our changes by testing with the original code on a single test.

input.description: Test with a simple non-conv test (relu) to rule out code issue

content.content: EXIT=1

toolUseResult.stdout: EXIT=1

content.text:
Even a simple relu test (which we didn't modify) fails with the same behavior. QEMU is broken in this environment — it's not related to our code changes.

**Summary of what was done:**

The build compiled cleanly with `-Wall -Werror`. The changes are:

1. **Removed `_v4_pf`, `_v2_pf`** functions + `PREFETCH_R` macro (651 lines) — benchmarked with no gain, never called
2. **Removed `_v2`** function (597 lines) — fully replaced by `_v4` which is strictly better (4x2 tiling vs 2x2) and handles all channel counts
3. **Replaced all `_v2` call sites** with `_v4` in 3 conv files (s4, 1xn variants)
4. **Removed header declarations** for `_v2`, `_v4_pf`, `_v2_pf`

Total: ~1250 lines of redundant code removed. Only `_v4` remains as the single GEMM kernel.

QEMU isn't working in the current environment (exits silently even for unrelated tests like relu), so functional verification will need to be done separately. Would you like to try running on the FPGA board instead?

content.text:
Found it! The semihosting file opens are failing:
```
openat(AT_FDCWD, "bin/8b_in1.bin", O_RDONLY) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
```

QEMU semihosting opens files relative to the **current working directory**. The test needs to run from `Examples/unit_func/` where the `bin/` directory with golden data lives. The run.sh script must `cd` there. Let me check what run.sh does.

content.content:
6:CWD=$(pwd)
37:APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
68:    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
70:    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
150:    cd ${CWD}
310:    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
311:    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"

content.text: So at line 310 it does `cd ../../Examples/unit_func/` then runs `./run.sh`. The problem is that my command was running from the wrong directory. Let me look at how `run.sh` in `Examples/unit_func/` invokes the wrapper.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}

content.text:
The issue is clear — the test runner `cd`s to `Examples/unit_func/` so that semihosting can find `bin/` files. But when I ran `run.sh` from the project directory, the inner script's `cd` worked correctly since it's part of the `run.sh` flow. The real issue is that my invocation ran correctly but QEMU still couldn't find files.

Wait — let me re-read the strace. The `run.sh` at line 310 does `cd ../../Examples/unit_func/`. But my command ran from `/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/`. So when the inner run.sh executes, the cwd should be `Examples/unit_func/`. Let me check what's really happening.

content.text:
Now I can see the issue. The wrapper script `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` uses `#!/bin/bash` as its shebang, but the sandbox doesn't have `bash` at `/bin/bash`. The wrapper is invoked as a subprocess by the test runner script, and when `bash` isn't found, it silently exits with code 1.

Let me verify:

content.content:
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1117048 Mar 12  2025 /bin/bash
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1117048 Mar 12  2025 /usr/bin/bash

toolUseResult.stdout:
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1117048 Mar 12  2025 /bin/bash
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1117048 Mar 12  2025 /usr/bin/bash

content.text: Bash exists. Let me try running the QEMU wrapper directly from the correct directory (where `bin/` golden data files exist).

input.description: Run QEMU from correct working directory

content.content: EXIT=0

toolUseResult.stdout: EXIT=0

content.text: Exit code 0 but no output visible. The QEMU `-nographic` output might go to a chardev that doesn't map to our stdout. Let me check the wrapper more carefully — it does `2>&1` redirect at the end.

input.description: Run wrapper with explicit bash and capture output

content.content: EXIT=0

toolUseResult.stdout: EXIT=0

content.content: 0 /tmp/qemu_test.txt

toolUseResult.stdout: 0 /tmp/qemu_test.txt

content.text: QEMU exited successfully but produced zero output. The semihosting output must be going somewhere else. Let me check if QEMU's chardev configuration routes output to a specific device.

content.text:
Found it! The semihosting writes go to **fd 9**, which is `EBADF` (bad file descriptor). QEMU's semihosting is trying to write to a file descriptor that doesn't exist. The test outputs "accuracy checking pass" — the tests themselves work fine, but the output is going to the wrong fd.

This is an Andes QEMU semihosting issue where the semihosting stdout maps to an internal fd (9) that isn't connected properly. The semihosting console output uses fd 1 (stdout) for the host process, but QEMU's semihosting implementation routes it to a different fd.

Let me check if there's a semihosting fd mapping or chardev option:

content.text:
So fd 9 is reused by semihosting for file opens — it's not a persistent fd for stdout. The semihosting `write` to fd 9 is the guest's fd (the guest-side file descriptor for `:tt` / stdout), but QEMU maps it to the host fd 9 which happens to be closed at that point.

This is a bug in the Andes QEMU semihosting: the host-side file descriptor for the semihosting console (`:tt` / stdout) isn't properly mapped. It should map to the QEMU process's fd 1 (stdout), but instead it's using some internal fd that gets closed.

Let me check if there's a workaround — maybe QEMU needs to keep certain fds open:

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# QEMU Validation Runner for libnn
     4→#
     5→# Compiles a single unit test against the existing libnn.a and runs it on QEMU
     6→# to verify correctness against golden data.
     7→#
     8→# Prerequisites: Run build_libnn.sh first to produce libnn.a
     9→#
    10→# Usage:
    11→#   ./run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [options]
    12→#
    13→# Examples:
    14→#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
    15→#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --vlen 1024
    16→#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
    17→#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --perf
    18→#
    19→# Environment:
    20→#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
    21→#   QEMU_SYS_RV64  - Path to QEMU binary (default: /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64)
    22→################################################################################
    23→set -euo pipefail
    24→
    25→#===== Defaults =====
    26→TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
    27→QEMU_SYS_RV64="${QEMU_SYS_RV64:-/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
    28→VLEN=512
    29→USE_STRACE=0
    30→PERF_ONLY=0
    31→TIMEOUT=120
    32→VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    33→
    34→#===== Parse arguments =====
    35→TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
    36→if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    37→    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--vlen N] [--strace] [--perf] [--timeout N]"
    38→    echo ""
    39→    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix)"
    40→    echo "  --vlen N      Vector length: 128, 256, 512 (default), 1024"
    41→    echo "  --strace      Use strace to capture output (needed in some environments)"
    42→    echo "  --perf        Performance mode: run only the large test case"
    43→    echo "  --timeout N   QEMU timeout in seconds (default: 120)"
    44→    exit 1
    45→fi
    46→shift
    47→
    48→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    49→    case "$1" in
    50→        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    51→        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
    52→        --perf)    PERF_ONLY=1; shift ;;
    53→        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    54→        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    55→    esac
    56→done
    57→
    58→#===== Resolve paths =====
    59→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    60→LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
    61→PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
    62→UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
    63→WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"
    64→
    65→export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
    66→CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"
    67→
    68→# Check libnn.a exists
    69→if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
    70→    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first."
    71→    exit 1
    72→fi
    73→
    74→# Select QEMU wrapper based on VLEN
    75→QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d${VLEN}_b512"
    76→if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    77→    QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d$((VLEN/2))_b512"
    78→fi
    79→if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    80→    echo "Error: No QEMU wrapper found for VLEN=${VLEN}"
    81→    ls "${PROJECT_DIR}"/riscv-qemu-wrapper-* 2>/dev/null
    82→    exit 1
    83→fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# QEMU Validation Runner for libnn
#
# Compiles a single unit test against the existing libnn.a and runs it on QEMU
# to verify correctness against golden data.
#
# Prerequisites: Run build_libnn.sh first to produce libnn.a
#
# Usage:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --vlen 1024
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace
#   ./run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --perf
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
#   QEMU_SYS_RV64  - Path to QEMU binary (default: /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
QEMU_SYS_RV64="${QEMU_SYS_RV64:-/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VLEN=512
USE_STRACE=0
PERF_ONLY=0
TIMEOUT=120
VEC_CFG="zve64d"

#===== Parse arguments =====
TEST_NAME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--vlen N] [--strace] [--perf] [--timeout N]"
    echo ""
    echo "  test_name     Unit test name (without t_ prefix)"
    echo "  --vlen N      Vector length: 128, 256, 512 (default), 1024"
    echo "  --strace      Use strace to capture output (needed in some environments)"
    echo "  --perf        Performance mode: run only the large test case"
    echo "  --timeout N   QEMU timeout in seconds (default: 120)"
    exit 1
fi
shift

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --perf)    PERF_ONLY=1; shift ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

# Check libnn.a exists
if [ ! -f "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first."
    exit 1
fi

# Select QEMU wrapper based on VLEN
QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d${VLEN}_b512"
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    QEMU_WRAPPER="${PROJECT_DIR}/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v${VLEN}_d$((VLEN/2))_b512"
fi
if [ ! -f "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]; then
    echo "Error: No QEMU wrapper found for VLEN=${VLEN}"
    ls "${PROJECT_DIR}"/riscv-qemu-wrapper-* 2>/dev/null
    exit 1
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "  QEMU Validation: t_${TEST_NAME}"
echo "============================================"
echo "  VLEN:    ${VLEN}"
echo "  Perf:    ${PERF_ONLY}"
echo "  Strace:  ${USE_STRACE}"
echo "  Wrapper: $(basename ${QEMU_WRAPPER})"
echo "============================================"

#===== Step 1: Compile test binary =====
echo ""
echo "[1/2] Compiling test binary..."
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
TEST_ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}_qemu.adx"

if [ ! -f "${TEST_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Test source not found: ${TEST_SRC}"
    exit 1
fi

${CC} -mvh \
    -Wall -Werror -O3 \
    -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
    -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER \
    -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mzfh \
    -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE \
    -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${PERF_ONLY} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
    -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -DENA_VL4 \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/Include/" \
    -I"${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include/" \
    -I"${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include/" \
    -o "${TEST_ADX}" \
    "${TEST_SRC}" \
    "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a" -lm 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: Compilation error"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: $(basename ${TEST_ADX})"

#===== Step 2: Run on QEMU =====
echo ""
echo "[2/2] Running on QEMU (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)..."

export QEMU_SYS_RV64
export VEC_CFG

cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"

if [ ${USE_STRACE} -eq 1 ]; then
    # Strace mode: captures semihosting output written to non-stdout fd.
    # Needed when QEMU's chardev fd isn't connected to the terminal (e.g. in
    # sandboxed environments like Claude Code).
    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/qemu_strace_${TEST_NAME}.txt"
    strace -f -e trace=write -o "${STRACE_LOG}" \
        timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1 || true

    # Extract semihosting output (writes to high fd numbers, skip internal eventfd on fd 8)
    echo ""
    echo "--- QEMU Output (via strace) ---"
    grep 'write([0-9]' "${STRACE_LOG}" | grep -v 'write(8,' | \
        sed -n 's/.*write([0-9]*, "\(.*\)", [0-9]*).*/\1/p' | \
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            echo -e "${line}"
        done
    echo "--- End ---"

    if grep -q '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '"accuracy checking pass' "${STRACE_LOG}")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif grep -q '"accuracy checking fail' "${STRACE_LOG}"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail found)"
        exit 2
    fi
else
    # Direct mode: works when the terminal properly connects QEMU chardev to stdout
    OUTPUT=$(timeout ${TIMEOUT} "${QEMU_WRAPPER}" "${TEST_ADX}" 2>&1) || true
    echo "${OUTPUT}"

    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking pass"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -c "accuracy checking pass")
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-test(s) passed)"
        exit 0
    elif echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -q "accuracy checking fail"; then
        echo ""
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo "WARNING: No output from QEMU. Try --strace mode."
        exit 2
    fi
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Build libnn for a specific CPU target
#
# Usage:
#   ./build_libnn.sh [options]
#
# Examples:
#   ./build_libnn.sh
#   ./build_libnn.sh --cpu ax45mpv --vec-cfg zve64d
#   ./build_libnn.sh --verbose
#
# Environment:
#   TOOLCHAIN_PATH  - Path to Andes toolchain bin/ (default: /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin)
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

#===== Defaults =====
TOOLCHAIN_PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH:-/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin}"
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
VEC_CFG="zve64d"
VERBOSE=0

#===== Parse arguments =====
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --cpu)     CPU_NAME="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --vec-cfg) VEC_CFG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --verbose) VERBOSE=1; shift ;;
        -h|--help)
            echo "Usage: $0 [--cpu NAME] [--vec-cfg CFG] [--verbose]"
            echo ""
            echo "  --cpu NAME     CPU build target (default: ax45mpv)"
            echo "  --vec-cfg CFG  Vector config: zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, zve64d (default: zve64d)"
            echo "  --verbose      Show full build output"
            exit 0
            ;;
        *) echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

#===== Resolve paths =====
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_PATH}:${LIB_ROOT}:${PATH}"
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"

LIB_CFLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing -I./Include -I./internal -mzfh"

echo "Building libnn for ${CPU_NAME} (${VEC_CFG})..."
cd "${LIB_ROOT}"

if [ ${VERBOSE} -eq 1 ]; then
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" 2>&1
else
    bash build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}" > /dev/null 2>&1
fi

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "FAILED: Library build error. Re-run with --verbose to see details."
    exit 1
fi

echo "OK: ${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
ls -lh "${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"

1→---
     2→name: qemu-validate
     3→description: "QEMU functional validation for libnn on Andes AX45MPV. Use when verifying correctness of libnn functions after code changes. Triggers on: validate, QEMU test, functional test, accuracy check, run tests, verify correctness, unit test."
     4→---
     5→
     6→# QEMU Functional Validation for libnn
     7→
     8→Build and run libnn unit tests on QEMU to verify functional correctness after code changes.
     9→
    10→## Environment Setup
    11→
    12→```bash
    13→export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
    14→export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Running All Unit Tests
    18→
    19→The full test suite runs 294 unit tests via the project run.sh script:
    20→
    21→```bash
    22→cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
    23→export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
    24→export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
    25→bash ./run.sh
    26→```
    27→
    28→- `run.sh` uses `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
    29→- Default VLEN=512 (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512`)
    30→- To change VLEN, edit `RUN_WRAPPER` in `run.sh` (line ~101)
    31→
    32→### Available QEMU VLEN configurations
    33→
    34→| Wrapper script | VLEN | DLEN | BLEN |
    35→|---|---|---|---|
    36→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128` | 128 | 128 | 128 |
    37→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128` | 256 | 128 | 128 |
    38→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256` | 256 | 256 | 256 |
    39→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256` | 512 | 256 | 256 |
    40→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` | 512 | 512 | 512 |
    41→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512` | 1024 | 512 | 512 |
    42→| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` | 1024 | 1024 | 512 |
    43→
    44→User's primary target: **VLEN=1024** (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`)
    45→
    46→## Running a Single Test
    47→
    48→To quickly validate a single function after editing:
    49→
    50→```bash
    51→export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
    52→export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
    53→
    54→# 1. Rebuild the library
    55→cd /home/nick/work/libnn
    56→build.sh AX45MPV
    57→
    58→# 2. Compile the test
    59→cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
    60→CC=riscv64-elf-gcc
    61→CFLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mvh -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE -mzfh -DENA_VL4"
    62→INC="-I../../Examples/unit_func/include -I../../Include -I../../internal"
    63→LIB="-L../../release -lnn_v -lm"
    64→FUNC_NAME="<function_name>"  # e.g., conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast_any
    65→$CC $CFLAGS $INC -o t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx ../../Examples/unit_func/t_${FUNC_NAME}.c $LIB
    66→
    67→# 3. Run via QEMU
    68→./riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx
    69→```
    70→
    71→Expected output contains `pass` for successful accuracy verification.
    72→
    73→## Workflow: Validate After Optimization
    74→
    75→Typical workflow after modifying a function's RVV implementation:
    76→
    77→1. **Rebuild library**: `cd /home/nick/work/libnn && build.sh AX45MPV`
    78→2. **Quick single-function test**: Compile and run the specific test via QEMU (see above)
    79→3. **Full regression**: Run all 294 tests via `run.sh` to check nothing else broke
    80→4. **Performance measurement**: Use the `fpga-bench` skill on FPGA board for cycle counts
    81→
    82→## Test Output Interpretation
    83→
    84→- `pass` — function output matches golden reference data
    85→- `fail` — output differs from golden data (accuracy regression)
    86→- No `pass`/`fail` — test binary failed to execute (build or QEMU issue)
    87→- Test golden data is in `Examples/unit_func/bin/`
    88→- Test source files follow `Examples/unit_func/t_<function_name>.c` naming
    89→
    90→## QEMU Details
    91→
    92→- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
    93→- Machine: `andes_ae350`
    94→- CPU model: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={128..1024}`
    95→- Semihosting enabled for stdio/file I/O
    96→- Config files: `project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/<vec_cfg>/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg`
    97→

---
name: qemu-validate
description: "QEMU functional validation for libnn on Andes AX45MPV. Use when verifying correctness of libnn functions after code changes. Triggers on: validate, QEMU test, functional test, accuracy check, run tests, verify correctness, unit test."
---

# QEMU Functional Validation for libnn

Build and run libnn unit tests on QEMU to verify functional correctness after code changes.

## Environment Setup

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
```

## Running All Unit Tests

The full test suite runs 294 unit tests via the project run.sh script:

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` uses `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default VLEN=512 (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512`)
- To change VLEN, edit `RUN_WRAPPER` in `run.sh` (line ~101)

### Available QEMU VLEN configurations

| Wrapper script | VLEN | DLEN | BLEN |
|---|---|---|---|
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128` | 128 | 128 | 128 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128` | 256 | 128 | 128 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256` | 256 | 256 | 256 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256` | 512 | 256 | 256 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` | 512 | 512 | 512 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512` | 1024 | 512 | 512 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` | 1024 | 1024 | 512 |

User's primary target: **VLEN=1024** (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`)

## Running a Single Test

To quickly validate a single function after editing:

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"

# 1. Rebuild the library
cd /home/nick/work/libnn
build.sh AX45MPV

# 2. Compile the test
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
CC=riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mvh -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE -mzfh -DENA_VL4"
INC="-I../../Examples/unit_func/include -I../../Include -I../../internal"
LIB="-L../../release -lnn_v -lm"
FUNC_NAME="<function_name>"  # e.g., conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast_any
$CC $CFLAGS $INC -o t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx ../../Examples/unit_func/t_${FUNC_NAME}.c $LIB

# 3. Run via QEMU
./riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx
```

Expected output contains `pass` for successful accuracy verification.

## Workflow: Validate After Optimization

Typical workflow after modifying a function's RVV implementation:

1. **Rebuild library**: `cd /home/nick/work/libnn && build.sh AX45MPV`
2. **Quick single-function test**: Compile and run the specific test via QEMU (see above)
3. **Full regression**: Run all 294 tests via `run.sh` to check nothing else broke
4. **Performance measurement**: Use the `fpga-bench` skill on FPGA board for cycle counts

## Test Output Interpretation

- `pass` — function output matches golden reference data
- `fail` — output differs from golden data (accuracy regression)
- No `pass`/`fail` — test binary failed to execute (build or QEMU issue)
- Test golden data is in `Examples/unit_func/bin/`
- Test source files follow `Examples/unit_func/t_<function_name>.c` naming

## QEMU Details

- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Machine: `andes_ae350`
- CPU model: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={128..1024}`
- Semihosting enabled for stdio/file I/O
- Config files: `project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/<vec_cfg>/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg`

The fix is to make the QEMU wrapper use strace to capture output. Let me create a wrapper that does this:

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

input.content:
---
name: QEMU semihosting requires strace in Claude Code
description: Andes QEMU semihosting writes to fd 9 (not stdout) in Claude Code's sandbox — use qemu-validate scripts with --strace, never run QEMU wrappers or run.sh directly
type: feedback
---

## Problem
In Claude Code's sandboxed environment, Andes QEMU's semihosting output (printf from guest code) is written to host fd 9 instead of stdout. This means:
- QEMU wrapper scripts produce zero output
- `run.sh` (project/ax45mpv/run.sh) reports 0% pass rate because `tee` captures nothing
- QEMU exits successfully (code 0) but all log files are empty

## Root Cause
QEMU's semihosting implementation maps the guest's stdout (`:tt`) to a host fd that ends up as fd 9 due to QEMU's internal fd allocation. In a normal terminal this fd is connected; in Claude Code's sandbox it isn't, causing `write(9, ...) = EBADF`.

## Solution: Use qemu-validate/ scripts
Always use the scripts in `qemu-validate/` directory with `--strace` mode:

```bash
# Build
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh

# Run single test
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> --strace --vlen 1024

# Examples:
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
```

The `--strace` mode uses `strace -f -e trace=write` to capture semihosting output from the fd and parses "accuracy checking pass/fail" from the strace log.

## DO NOT use
- `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` directly — it will show 0% pass rate
- QEMU wrapper scripts directly (e.g. `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512`) — no output
- Running QEMU binary directly — same issue

toolUseResult.content:
---
name: QEMU semihosting requires strace in Claude Code
description: Andes QEMU semihosting writes to fd 9 (not stdout) in Claude Code's sandbox — use qemu-validate scripts with --strace, never run QEMU wrappers or run.sh directly
type: feedback
---

## Problem
In Claude Code's sandboxed environment, Andes QEMU's semihosting output (printf from guest code) is written to host fd 9 instead of stdout. This means:
- QEMU wrapper scripts produce zero output
- `run.sh` (project/ax45mpv/run.sh) reports 0% pass rate because `tee` captures nothing
- QEMU exits successfully (code 0) but all log files are empty

## Root Cause
QEMU's semihosting implementation maps the guest's stdout (`:tt`) to a host fd that ends up as fd 9 due to QEMU's internal fd allocation. In a normal terminal this fd is connected; in Claude Code's sandbox it isn't, causing `write(9, ...) = EBADF`.

## Solution: Use qemu-validate/ scripts
Always use the scripts in `qemu-validate/` directory with `--strace` mode:

```bash
# Build
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh

# Run single test
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> --strace --vlen 1024

# Examples:
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
```

The `--strace` mode uses `strace -f -e trace=write` to capture semihosting output from the fd and parses "accuracy checking pass/fail" from the strace log.

## DO NOT use
- `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` directly — it will show 0% pass rate
- QEMU wrapper scripts directly (e.g. `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512`) — no output
- Running QEMU binary directly — same issue

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

libnn is the Andes Neural Network Library — a C library of optimized NN operators for RISC-V and NDS32 architectures. It provides multiple implementation variants per function: plain C (portable reference), DSP-optimized (RISC-V P-extension), and Vector-optimized (RVV 0.8 and 1.0). It maintains a CMSIS-NN compatibility layer for ARM portability.

## Build Commands

```bash
# Build the library for a specific CPU target
./build.sh <CPU_NAME> [EXTRA_FLAGS] [COMPILER] [OUTPUT_PATH]

# Examples:
./build.sh D25                          # RV32 with DSP
./build.sh NX27V                        # RV64 with vector extension
./build.sh AX45MPV                      # RV64 with vector, 45-series tuning
./build.sh x64                          # Native x86-64 build (for host testing)

# Supported CPUs: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27,
#   N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, x86, x64
```

Output: `libnn.a` static library + `libnn.objdump` disassembly.

## Testing

```bash
# Run tests from a separate build directory:
mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir

# Plain C algorithm with GDB simulator
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3

# DSP algorithm with RV32 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

# Vector extension (RVV 1.0) with RV64 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"

# DAILY mode (includes code coverage via gcov)
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <target> DAILY [flags]
```

Unit tests are in `Examples/unit_func/t_*.c` (~301 test files). Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/`.

## Architecture

### Source Organization

All source lives under `Source/` in 9 function categories:
- **ConvolutionFunctions/** — conv1x1, convHWC, depthwise conv, grouped conv, transposed conv
- **FullyConnectedFunctions/** — fully connected layers
- **ActivationFunctions/** — relu, sigmoid, tanh, silu, leaky relu, etc.
- **PoolingFunctions/** — average/max pooling
- **SoftmaxFunctions/** — softmax variants
- **BasicFunctions/** — elementwise add, multiply
- **ConcatenationFunctions/** — tensor concatenation
- **NNSupportFunctions/** — data conversion, requantization, buffer helpers
- **UtilFunctions/** — NMS, decode_dist2box, focal loss, etc.

### Function Naming Convention

```
riscv_nn_<operation>_<layout>_<input_type>_<weight_type>_<output_type>_<quant_mode>_<suffix>
```

Example: `riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any`
- `conv_1x1_HWC` — 1x1 convolution in HWC layout
- `s8_s8_s8` — signed 8-bit input, weight, output
- `asym` — asymmetric quantization (`sym` = symmetric, `sft` = shift-based)
- `bias` — includes bias addition
- `fast_any` — optimized variant, any channel count

Data types: `s4`, `s8`, `s16`, `f16`, `f32`, `u8`

### Multi-variant Implementation Pattern

Each function has up to 3 implementation paths selected at compile time via preprocessor guards:

1. **Vector (RVV)** — `#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA` with version checks via `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION`
2. **DSP** — `#ifdef NDS_CPU_64` / P-extension intrinsics
3. **Plain C** — fallback, always present

Vector internal headers are versioned: `internal_vec_isa_v0_8.h`, `internal_vec_isa_v1_0.h`, etc.

### Headers

- **Public API** (`Include/`): `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_activation.h`, `riscv_nn_types.h`, etc.
- **Internal** (`internal/`): `internal_nn_math.h` (math macros/inline helpers), `internal_dsp_isa.h` (DSP intrinsics), `internal_vec_*.h` (vector intrinsics)

### CMSIS-NN Compatibility

`cmsis_include/` provides adapter headers (`convert_between_andes_nn_and_cmsis_nn.h`) mapping ARM CMSIS-NN APIs to libnn equivalents. Supports CMSIS-NN 3.0, 3.2, and 4.1.

## Commit Message Convention

Prefix with category tag in brackets:
- `[opt/rvv]` — RISC-V vector extension optimization
- `[opt/dsp]` — DSP extension optimization
- `[bug-fix]` — Bug fix
- `[new fun]` — New function added
- `[fine-tune]` — Algorithm tuning
- `[misc]` — Maintenance/cleanup

## Compilation Flags

Key defines that control code paths:
- `-DENA_VEC_ISA` — Enable vector ISA code paths
- `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80|1000` — Select RVV version (0.8 or 1.0)
- `-DMAX_VLEN=512|1024` — Maximum vector length
- `-DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — Enable vector dot product instructions
- `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` — Auto-set for 45-series CPU tuning
- `-DENA_ACE_RVV` — Enable ACE (Andes Customization Engine) extensions

Base flags: `-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror`

input.new_string:
Unit tests are in `Examples/unit_func/t_*.c` (~301 test files). Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/`.

### QEMU Validation (preferred for Claude Code)

**IMPORTANT:** In Claude Code's sandbox, Andes QEMU's semihosting output goes to fd 9 instead of stdout. The standard `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` and QEMU wrapper scripts will produce zero output and report 0% pass rate. Always use the `qemu-validate/` scripts with `--strace` mode instead.

```bash
# Build libnn
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh

# Run a single test (always use --strace in Claude Code)
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> --strace --vlen 1024

# Examples:
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
```

The `--strace` mode uses strace to capture semihosting writes and parses "accuracy checking pass/fail" from the trace. Tests can run in parallel for independent functions.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
[misc] Remove redundant GEMM kernels — consolidate v2/v2_pf/v4_pf into v4

Remove nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2, _v4_pf, _v2_pf and the
PREFETCH_R macro, keeping only _v4 as the single GEMM kernel.

- v4 is strictly superior to v2 (4x2 tiling vs 2x2, 3.09x vs 2.47x)
- v4 handles all channel counts (4-ch main loop + 2-ch + 1-ch remainder)
- v4 already fell back to v2 in non-VEC builds; now uses the same stub
- v4_pf and v2_pf (software prefetch variants) showed no gain on AX45MPV
- All call sites in conv_HWC_s8_s8_s4, conv_1xn_s8/s4 updated v2 -> v4

~1250 lines of dead/redundant code removed. Verified on QEMU (VLEN=1024):
  conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any:     3/3 pass
  conv_HWC_s8_s8_s4_asym_bias_any:     5/5 pass
  conv_1xn_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any: 3/3 pass
  conv_1xn_HWC_s8_s8_s4_asym_bias_any: 5/5 pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit the consolidated GEMM kernel changes
